column1: #mapping_set_description: Biospecimen_fields-level mappings from ncihtan/data-models@v25.2.1 to ncihtan/htan2-data-model@v1.3.0

column8: Identical field for adjacent biospecimen identifiers.
Identical primary key identifier in both models.
Identical foreign key identifier in both models.
Both capture collection timing. HTAN2 uses age in days; HTAN1 uses days from index.

Both fields capture ICD-O-3 morphology codes but reference different caDSR IDs: source caDSR:3226275 vs target caDSR:11326261. The HTAN2 caDSR ID is a newer/updated version of the same concept. Confirmed as skos:closeMatch (not exactMatch) because the caDSR IDs differ; verified during QA review.
Both capture processing timing. HTAN2 uses age in days; HTAN1 uses days from index.
Both capture sectioning timing. HTAN2 uses age in days; HTAN1 uses days from index.
Both capture timepoint data for specimen collection.
Both classify tumor as Primary/Metastatic/Recurrent. Different caDSR IDs but same concept.
Both capture tissue sample type but HTAN2 is more specific (Block/Section) while HTAN1 is broader.
HTAN1 uses generic numbered dimensions; HTAN2 uses named longest/shortest. Dim 1 conventionally = longest.
HTAN1 uses generic numbered dimensions; HTAN2 uses named shortest. Dim 3 conventionally = shortest.
Fixative Type is a narrower concept than Preservation Medium: fixatives are one specific category of preservation media. Changed from closeMatch to narrowMatch during QA review.

Ischemic temperature (pre-preservation, warm/cold ischemia) is a distinct concept from preservation method temperature (storage temperature during preservation). These fields measure temperature in different biological phases. Retained as skos:relatedMatch at 0.5 after QA review; the connection is tenuous and implementers should verify whether a mapping is appropriate for their use case.
Both assess cellular patterns but different scope: architecture vs preinvasive morphology.
